channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: A landmark climate change case will open at the top UN court as island nations fear rising seas
item.description: THE HAGUE (AP) — The top United Nations court will take up the largest case in its history on Monday, when it opens two weeks of hearings into what countries worldwide are legally required to do to combat climate change and help vulnerable nations fight its devastating impact.
item.dc:creator: MOLLY QUELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Negotiators fail to reach an agreement on a plastic pollution treaty. Talks to resume next year
item.description: BUSAN, South Korea (AP) — Negotiators working on a treaty to address the global crisis of plastic pollution for a week in South Korea won’t reach an agreement and plan to resume the talks next year.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER McDERMOTT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Largest gold deposit in the world worth $83 billion found in China
item.description: A discovery of gold reserves, estimated to be worth $83 billion, was made in China last month. China is the world's largest gold producer, Reuters reports.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Georgian PM praises country's protest crackdown despite US condemnation
item.description: Georgia, which has been criticized for using "excessive force" to crack down on anti-government protests, has become increasingly authoritarian, according to Western governments.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Tiffany Trump's father-in-law Massad Boulos to become senior presidential adviser on Arab affairs
item.description: ​President-elect Trump on Sunday said he would appoint Massad Boulos, a billionaire from Lebanon whose son Michael is married to Tiffany Trump, to serve as his senior adviser on Arab and Middle Eastern affairs.
item.dc:creator: Ian Swanson, The Hill

item.title: Israeli strikes hit southern Lebanon, but tense ceasefire holds
item.description: Lebanon’s state-run National News Agency says Israeli jets have launched an airstrike over a southern Lebanese border village, while troops shelled other border towns and villages still under Israeli control.
item.dc:creator: Kareem Chehayeb And Fadi Tawil, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: A new Belgium law gives sex workers contracts, benefits and safety protection
item.description: Belgium is granting sex workers full labor rights, including health insurance, paid leave and pensions, starting Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Tom Nouvian, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Social democrats gain and incumbents are punished in Iceland's election
item.description: REYKJAVIK, Iceland (AP) — Voters in Iceland joined a global trend of punishing incumbents in a parliamentary election, with a center-left party winning the largest share of votes in the North Atlantic island nation.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ireland election: Incumbent center-right parties likely to retain power, but left-wing Sinn Fein holds water
item.description: Ireland's election results have yet to be finalized, but exit polling suggests a coalition government between the center-right Fine Gael and Fianna Fáil.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Over 40 people hospitalized in Georgia during protests over the suspension of EU talks
item.description: A third night of protests in the Georgian capital against the government’s decision to suspend negotiations to join the European Union has left 44 people hospitalized.
item.dc:creator: Sophiko Megrelidze, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Ireland's incumbent parties look likely to hang on to power after a fractured election
item.description: Results from Ireland's election suggest the two dominant center-right parties look likely to form a new government.
item.dc:creator: Jill Lawless And Peter Morrison, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: UN halts aid shipments through Gaza's main crossing after looting. It blames the crisis on Israel
item.description: The U.N. agency for Palestinian refugees says it is halting aid deliveries through the main cargo crossing into Gaza because of the threat of armed gangs who have looted recent convoys.
item.dc:creator: Wafaa Shurafa And Samy Magdy, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Protesters gather for third night of demonstrations after Georgia announces suspension of EU talks
item.description: TBILISI, Georgia (AP) — Protesters gathered across Georgia on Saturday night in a third straight night of demonstrations against the government’s decision to suspend negotiations to join the European Union.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIKO MEGRELIDZE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hamas releases new hostage video of American-Israeli Edan Alexander
item.description: Hamas released a propaganda video Saturday showing the 20-year-old Alexander alive, marking his first proof of life since being kidnapped.
item.dc:creator: Mills Hayes, NewsNation

item.title: Iceland votes for a new parliament after political disagreements force an early election
item.description: REYKJAVIK, Iceland (AP) — Icelanders voted to elect a new parliament Saturday after disagreements over immigration, energy policy and the economy forced Prime Minister Bjarni Benediktsson to pull the plug on his coalition government and call an early election.
item.dc:creator: MARCO DI MARCO and DANICA KIRKA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Counting is underway in Ireland's election as 3 parties battle for top place
item.description: DUBLIN (AP) — A marathon vote-counting exercise was underway Saturday in Ireland’s national election after an exit poll suggested that the contest is a close-fought race among the country’s three largest political parties.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS and PETER MORRISON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: An Israeli strike in Gaza kills World Central Kitchen workers. Israel says 1 was an Oct. 7 attacker
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — An Israeli airstrike on a car in the Gaza Strip on Saturday killed five people, including employees of World Central Kitchen.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA, TIA GOLDENBERG and BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: How Brazilian police say Bolsonaro plotted a coup to stay in office
item.description: Brazil's Federal Police in November formally accused former President Jair Bolsonaro and more than 30 other people of attempting a coup to keep him in office.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Regional Spanish leader who botched flood response faces another big march demanding his resignation
item.description: VALENCIA, Spain (AP) — Tens of thousands of Spaniards marched on Saturday in the streets of Valencia a month after devastating floods to demand the resignation of the regional officials who bungled the emergency response.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Stealth destroyer to be home for 1st hypersonic weapon on a US warship
item.description: The U.S. Navy is transforming a costly flub into a potent weapon with the first shipborne hypersonic weapon, which is being retrofitted aboard the first of its three stealthy destroyers.
item.dc:creator: DAVID SHARP, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump lauds 'productive' meeting with Trudeau amid tension over tariffs
item.description: President-elect Trump confirmed his Friday meeting with Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau, calling the conversation "productive.
item.dc:creator: Steff Danielle Thomas, The Hill

item.title: Former Israeli diplomat: Israel-Hamas ceasefire not likely before Biden leaves
item.description: Former Israeli diplomat Alon Pinkas predicted Saturday that a ceasefire agreement between Israel and Palestinian militant group Hamas in Gaza is unlikely before President Biden departs the White House early next year.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Islamist rebels in Syria catch Assad, Putin, Iran regimes off guard giving US new mideast headache
item.description: Amid a rapid-fire collapse of Syrian, Hezbollah, Russian and Iranian forces in northern Syria, questions are surfacing about U.S. policy toward the emerging new order in the war-ravaged Syrian Arab Republic.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israeli military says Hezbollah is smuggling weapons into Lebanon during ceasefire
item.description: Hezbollah terrorists reportedly are smuggling weapons across Lebanon's borders despite an ongoing ceasefire with Israel.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel hits alleged Hezbollah weapons smuggling sites in Syria
item.description: Israeli military jets hit alleged Hezbollah weapons smuggling sites near the border crossing between Lebanon and Syria, according to officials, who allege the Lebanese militant group violated a ceasefire agreement.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Taiwan's president arrives in Hawaii for a 2-day stop in the US as part of South Pacific visit
item.description: Taiwan’s President Lai Ching-te has arrived in Hawaii for a two-day transit in the U.S. as part of a trip to the South Pacific.
item.dc:creator: Taijing Wu And Huizhong Wu, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: What to know about sudden rebel gains in Syria's 13-year war and why it matters
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The 13-year civil war in Syria has roared back into prominence with a surprise rebel offensive on Aleppo, one of Syria's largest cities and an ancient business hub.
item.dc:creator: ELLEN KNICKMEYER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump wants a Gaza ceasefire deal 'now,' Graham says
item.description: Sen. Lindsey Graham (R-S.C.) said that President-elect Trump wants to reach a ceasefire and hostage deal before inauguration day on Jan. 20, Axios reported on Friday. "Trump is more determined than ever to release the hostages and supports a ceasefire that includes a hostage deal.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, The Hill

item.title: Georgian protesters clash with police for a second night after EU talks are suspended
item.description: TBILISI, Georgia (AP) — Thousands of demonstrators protesting the Georgian government's decision to suspend negotiations to join the European Union rallied outside the parliament and clashed with police for a second straight night on Friday.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIKO MEGRELIDZE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What Trump's Russia-Ukraine envoy has said about ending the war
item.description: President-elect Trump's choice for special envoy to the Russia-Ukraine war, Keith Kellogg, has offered fairly detailed proposals for ending the war that began almost three years ago.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: In a shock offensive, insurgents breach Syria's largest city for the first time since 2016
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Insurgents breached Syria’s largest city Friday and clashed with government forces for the first time since 2016, according to a war monitor and fighters, in a surprise attack that sent residents fleeing and added fresh uncertainty to a region reeling from multiple wars.
item.dc:creator: SARAH EL DEEB, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: British lawmakers move closer to allowing terminally ill adults to end their lives
item.description: Members of Parliament approved the assisted dying bill by a 330-275 vote, signaling their approval in principle for the bill, which will undergo further scrutiny before it goes to a final vote.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Canadian news publishers sue OpenAI over alleged copyright infringement
item.description: OTTAWA, Ontario (AP) — A coalition of Canadian news publishers, including The Canadian Press, Torstar, Globe and Mail, Postmedia and CBC/Radio-Canada, has filed a lawsuit against OpenAI for using news content to train its ChatGPT generative artificial intelligence system.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Hamas attacker opens fire at Israeli bus in West Bank, injuring at least 8: report
item.description: Hamas is claiming responsibility Friday for an attack on a bus near an Israeli settlement in the West Bank, which has left at least eight injured, reports say.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Democrats sound alarm on West Bank under Trump administration
item.description: House Democrats are sounding early alarms that a second Trump administration will lead to a vast erosion of Palestinian rights and undermine efforts to bring peace to the volatile Middle East.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, The Hill

item.title: Millions in Ukraine without power after Russia attacks grid
item.description: “Attacks on energy facilities are happening all over Ukraine,” Energy Minister Herman Halushchenko said on Facebook.
item.dc:creator: Libbey Dean, NewsNation

item.title: Biden team briefed Trump transition on Israel-Hezbollah ceasefire
item.description: President-elect Trump's transition team has received briefings from the chief architect of the Israel-Hezbollah ceasefire deal under President Biden, a senior administration official said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, NewsNation
